label: Day 1 — Charlottesville → Forrest City, AR
summary: Departed evening June 16. Down I-81. Foggy river crossing somewhere west of Nashville at first light. Memphis Bass Pro Pyramid mid-day. Roland, Oklahoma. EV charging stop in Sayre. First night at a rest area in the Texas Panhandle — pizza on the hood, sunset, crepuscular rays, big flat sky.
highlight: 009.jpg — Beautiful sunset at the Texas Panhandle rest area. First night sky of the trip.

label: Day 2 — Forrest City to Gray County, TX (and into Arizona)
summary: Left the Texas Panhandle and pushed west through Amarillo and across New Mexico. Flagstaff bypass. Pulled over at Meteor Crater Rest Area, Arizona, for the night — red rock formations at golden hour, a double rainbow, the snakes-and-insects sign, the Subaru holding still in the orange Arizona dusk.
highlight: 015.jpg — Rainbow over red rock formations at dusk, Meteor Crater Rest Area, AZ.

label: Day 3 — Arizona to the Eastern Sierra
summary: Woke up at Two Guns, AZ. I-40 west to Kingman — In-N-Out there. US-93 north through the desert, then Las Vegas, then US-95 north. Stopped briefly in Tonopah. Sunset at Millers Rest Area, Nevada — then a short run back to Tonopah and back. Stayed up for the kangaroo rat and the Milky Way.
highlight: 023.jpg — Subaru at sunset, Millers Rest Area, NV. Mountains holding the last light.

label: Day 4 — Owens Valley · Independence · Goldstone
summary: Left Millers and ran south on US-395 through the Owens Valley. Brief stop in Bishop. Independence — the turnaround, same as every year, touch the wall. Manzanar was packed, skipped it. High California gas prices. South through the Mojave, gas in Ludlow. I-40 east into Arizona — spotted a Victor Valley Transit bus near Barstow, and Area 66 in Yucca from the highway. Overnighted again at Two Guns.
highlight: 028.jpg — Road south toward the Sierra Nevada wall, approaching the Owens Valley. The view you come all this way for.

label: Day 5 — Monument Valley
summary: Left Two Guns. US-89 north through the red desert — Cameron, AZ. Then US-160 east to Kayenta, US-163 north. The Mittens appeared — West Mitten, East Mitten — exact as the photographs you've seen, somehow still not the same as being there. Continued north through Mexican Hat and into Bluff, UT. Then east toward Shiprock, NM, south to Gallup, and back onto I-40 east.
highlight: 042.jpg — East and West Mitten Buttes from US-163. Monument Valley. The planned stop that delivered.

label: Day 6 — Gray County to Forrest City (return)
summary: The mirror of Day 2. Same road, direction reversed. Oklahoma and Arkansas going the other way. The western life receding in the rearview. Forrest City Rest Area again for the night — same parking lot, different direction.
highlight: 049.jpg — Nighttime rest area, Forrest City, AR. The return leg. Same place, heading the other way.

label: Day 7 — Forrest City to Crossville, TN
summary: Memphis crossing on the return — the I-40 bridge, the Pyramid from a different angle than the outbound. Tennessee rolling by. Buc-ee's in Crossville. The mountains ahead, home somewhere past them.
highlight: 052.jpg — Bass Pro Shops Pyramid from the I-40 bridge, Memphis, TN. Same landmark, other direction.

label: Day 8 — Home
summary: Final overnight push. The Shenandoah in the dark. Charlottesville at 1am. Eight days, I-40 west and back. Trip #5.
highlight: 054.mp4 — Dashcam on the final overnight run, Tennessee into Virginia. Home in the dark.
